stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.text:
For AskCallback, I feel like it violates obp the naming, and how it should really be, I think.

I would think it would be like this, 'ask user "name?"' -> this gets funnelled to stream, then if the stream is stateless we sould create the callstack on it and then serialize it, if it is statefull we just serialize it. Lets disucss
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.anchor: Stage 2a fixes all four for **any** callback kind. `AskCallback` gets a real mid-goal continuation it never had. Stage 2b builds `PermissionAskCallback` on top of this foundation.
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.author: user

stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.text: I think we can do this, it is only working with stream, not touching on http. plus, isn´t it just serialize just include the callback?
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.anchor: **Out of scope:** building the HTTP/Web channel. The serializer is generic; whatever channel wires it (CLI today; HTTP when shipped — see `Channel/Message/this.cs:7` "Web extends Message (when shipped)") is a separate branch's concern. Stage 2a makes the bytes correct; the channel can use them whenever it lands.
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.author: user

stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.text: this is useless method FromEnvelope. What is Envelope? you are just returning data, data is just data, just send it around, when we want to serizlize data, then the signature is created, and it the Data class that does that on serialize
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.author: user
